name: Guangzhou
summary: Guangzhou is generally a safe destination for tourists with low violent crime rates and excellent public transport. Main concerns include petty theft, tourist scams, and typhoon season from February to September.
quickVerdict: With its robust metro system, strong police presence, and reputation for cultural tolerance, Guangzhou offers a relatively safe experience for tourists. The city maintains low violent crime rates and provides excellent public transportation infrastructure. Visitors should remain vigilant against pickpockets in crowded areas and be aware of common tourist scams, particularly around taxi services and currency exchange. The main natural risk is typhoon season from February to September, when weather conditions can significantly impact travel plans.

neighborhoods.name: Tianhe District
neighborhoods.description: Modern CBD with excellent lighting, heavy police presence, upscale shopping malls, and the Canton Tower. Very safe day and night with good metro connections.

neighborhoods.name: Yuexiu District
neighborhoods.description: Historic city center with major attractions like Chen Clan Ancestral Hall. Well-patrolled tourist area with good safety infrastructure and metro access.

neighborhoods.name: Liwan District
neighborhoods.description: Traditional area with Shamian Island's colonial architecture. Generally safe but exercise normal precautions in older residential areas after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Haizhu District
neighborhoods.description: Mixed commercial and residential area with good transport links. Safe during the day but some areas can be quieter at night.

neighborhoods.name: Baiyun District
neighborhoods.description: Large suburban district that can be less safe at night, particularly around the airport area. Some reports of petty crime in certain neighborhoods.

neighborhoods.name: Industrial Areas
neighborhoods.description: Manufacturing zones in outer districts with limited lighting and security. Avoid at night and exercise heightened caution during the day.

scams.name: Fake Currency Exchange
scams.description: Taxi drivers and shop assistants switch real money for counterfeit notes when giving change, claiming your money is fake to demand more payment.
scams.howToAvoid: Only exchange money at banks and official ATMs. Carefully inspect all currency received and keep an eye on money during transactions.

scams.name: Taxi Meter Fraud
scams.description: Drivers refuse to use meters, quote flat rates, or use tampered meters that run fast. Some unlicensed taxis operate at transport hubs.
scams.howToAvoid: Insist on meter use by saying 'da biao'. Use ride-hailing apps like DiDi for transparent pricing. Avoid unofficial taxi offers at airports.

scams.name: Tea House Scam
scams.description: Friendly strangers invite tourists for tea or to visit special shops, then pressure them into buying overpriced goods or services.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline unsolicited invitations from strangers. Never follow people to unfamiliar commercial establishments, especially if they seem overly insistent.

scams.name: Attraction Closed Scam
scams.description: Taxi drivers or touts claim your planned destination is closed and offer to take you to alternative locations that pay them commission.
scams.howToAvoid: Verify attraction hours independently. Make your own way to destinations and book through reputable sources rather than trusting random recommendations.

soloFemale.overview: Guangzhou is considered very safe for solo female travelers, with excellent safety ratings comparable to major international cities. The city has low rates of harassment and violent crime against women. Chinese culture places high value on respecting women, and curious looks from locals are typically benevolent rather than threatening. The metro system has female-only security check lanes and the city maintains good lighting and police presence.
tips: Stay in well-lit, populated areas especially at night
Use official transportation like metro or licensed taxis rather than unlicensed vehicles
Keep accommodation address written in Chinese characters for easy communication
Avoid empty areas alone at night, particularly in suburban districts
Download translation apps and carry emergency contact numbers

nightSafety.overview: Guangzhou maintains good night safety with well-lit main streets, active nightlife areas, and regular police patrols. Most tourist areas remain bustling with activity well into the evening. The metro system operates late and shopping districts stay illuminated. However, visitors should exercise standard urban precautions and avoid poorly lit areas or quiet streets after dark.
tips: Stick to well-lit, busy streets and tourist areas
Use metro or official taxis rather than walking alone in quiet areas
Avoid Baiyun District at night due to occasional petty crime reports
Stay aware of surroundings and keep valuables secure
Return to accommodation via main roads rather than back alleys

transport.metro: Guangzhou Metro is excellent with 19+ lines covering the city, English signage, security checks, and clean facilities. Operates roughly 6 AM to midnight with fares from 2-10 yuan. Very safe and efficient.
transport.rideshare: DiDi is widely available and safer than regular taxis with upfront pricing, GPS tracking, and English interface. Authorities actively combat fraudulent software used by some drivers.
transport.taxis: Generally safe and government-regulated, but insist on meter use. Some unlicensed taxis operate at transport hubs - avoid these. Official taxis are recognizable and affordable.
transport.tips: Metro is the safest option for tourists. For taxis, always demand meter use and have destinations written in Chinese. DiDi offers most transparency and safety features for ride-hailing.

customs: Tipping is not customary and can be considered rude in most situations - service staff do their job without expectation of extra payment
Greet elders and authority figures with respect, offering slight nods or bows as appropriate
Remove shoes when entering homes and accept offered slippers
Present gifts with both hands and expect initial polite refusal before acceptance
Avoid public displays of affection, loud behavior, or pointing with chopsticks

health.overview: Major hospitals provide good care but English-speaking staff may be limited outside international facilities. Air quality can be poor on some days. Food safety is generally good at established restaurants.
health.water: Tap water is not safe to drink - use bottled or boiled water. Hotels typically provide electric kettles and bottled water. Restaurants serve boiled water on request.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required. Standard travel vaccines recommended including hepatitis A/B and typhoid. Consult healthcare provider for current recommendations.
health.altitude: Sea level location with humid subtropical climate. Hot, humid summers and mild winters. Air pollution can occasionally reach unhealthy levels.

emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Consulate General Guangzhou: +86-20-3814-5775, located at No. 43 Huajiu Road, Zhujiang New Town, Tianhe District

faq.a: Yes, Guangzhou is generally very safe for tourists with low violent crime rates and good infrastructure. Most visitors find it safer than many cities of similar size worldwide, though normal urban precautions should be taken.

Generally yes, especially in tourist areas and business districts which remain well-lit and patrolled. However, avoid poorly lit areas and suburban districts like Baiyun at night. Main streets and metro areas are quite safe.

faq.q: Is Guangzhou safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. Guangzhou has excellent safety ratings for solo female travelers with minimal harassment reports. Chinese culture emphasizes respect for women and the city has good lighting, police presence, and safe public transport.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Guangzhou?
faq.a: Exercise caution in Baiyun District at night and avoid industrial areas in outer districts after dark. Otherwise, most tourist areas are safe, though standard urban awareness is recommended in crowded places.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Guangzhou?
faq.a: Yes, very safe. The metro system is excellent with security checks, English signage, and regular cleaning. Buses and licensed taxis are also generally safe, though insist on meter use for taxis.
